meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to master version control and ship faster as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: Stop manually copying files. By implementing a standardized SaaS starter kit, you integrate Git-based version control into your workflow instantly. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to automate commit history, ensuring every client project is robust, rollback-ready, and professional from the very first line of code.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a folder titled 'Final_v2_REAL_FINAL.' Your client just requested a change, and you realize you have no way to revert to last week's stable build without obliterating your current progress. This is the silent killer of freelance careers: the total absence of version control in your development lifecycle.

The industry doesn't care how fast you code if you can't manage your changes. A professional SaaS starter kit isn't just code; it's an insurance policy. By adopting a structured boilerplate, you shift from 'hacking together scripts' to 'engineering scalable products.' Let’s turn your chaotic local folders into a production-grade development environment.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean folder structure that separates core logic from configuration. This prevents the 'spaghetti code' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust SaaS project structure with separate directories for /src, /api, /components, and /tests, ensuring a clean separation of concerns for a production-ready application.

steps.title: Automate Version Control Hookups
steps.description: Configure Git to track changes automatically so you never lose a feature update again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standard .gitignore file optimized for modern SaaS stacks and initialize a local Git repository with a README.md file.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial project scaffold'

steps.title: Implement Environment Management
steps.description: Use environment variables to keep secrets out of your code, ensuring your repo remains secure and portable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example template with placeholders for API keys, database URLs, and authentication tokens.

steps.title: Standardize Build Scripts
steps.description: Create repeatable CLI commands so you can deploy your client's project with a single keystroke.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a package.json file containing standardized scripts for 'dev', 'build', 'test', and 'deploy' to ensure consistent project execution.

steps.title: Finalize CI/CD Integration
steps.description: Automate your deployment pipeline so your code is always tested and pushed to production without manual errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs unit tests on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always commit granularly: One feature, one commit. It makes rolling back significantly easier when a client hates a specific change.
Use branching strategies for client feedback; never commit experimental code directly to the main branch.
Automate your changelogs using Claude Code to extract commit messages, making you look like a seasoned DevOps engineer to your clients.
Keep a 'master' starter kit repo that you fork for every new project; update this master repo whenever you learn a better coding pattern.
Use Git tags to mark 'Milestone' deliveries for clients, giving them a clear record of progress and completed features.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is built, push it to a private GitHub repository, create a template, and use it to shave 10+ hours off your next client project.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint to stop the version control nightmare. Elevate your freelance game, ship faster, and stop losing sleep over overwritten files. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
